ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term for sesame oil chicken; mapped to broader 'chicken dish' class. Specific sesame oil chicken preparation not represented in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific code for sesame oil chicken dish; A0CKM (cooked chicken meat) used as the closest defensible anchor. Dish includes sesame oil, rice wine, ginger, and soy sauce.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept identified for sesame oil chicken as a prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of sesame oil chicken is approximately 200g, representing a single-person portion as served in a Singaporean home meal or hawker setting.

nutrient_source.title: Sesame Oil Chicken — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Sesame Oil Chicken (F11020047). All core macros and secondary nutrients are from this canonical entry with confidence 0.95.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — analogous estimate based on braised chicken with mixed seasonings
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition, 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for sesame oil chicken. GI estimated at 35 (low) based on analogous braised chicken dishes with soy sauce and mixed seasonings from the University of Sydney GI Database. The dish is predominantly protein and fat with modest carbohydrate (5.41g/100g) from soy sauce, rice wine, and ginger; this profile is consistent with a low GI. Glycemic load per 200g serving = (35 × 5.41 × 2) / 100 = 3.8g, classified as low GL.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 701mg per 100g — substantially exceeds the 600mg threshold for hypertension management
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid sesame oil chicken due to its high sodium content of 701mg/100g, primarily from soy sauce; if consumed, portion should be restricted to no more than 80g per meal and preparation should use low-sodium soy sauce to reduce sodium load below 560mg per serving.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Modest carbohydrate (5.41g/100g) and high protein (16.87g/100g) support glycaemic stability, but high sodium and alcohol content require caution
type2_diabetes.guidance: Sesame oil chicken has a low glycaemic index and modest carbohydrate content that supports glycaemic stability in individuals with Type 2 Diabetes; however, the high sodium content (701mg/100g) increases cardiovascular risk in this population, and rice wine alcohol content may disrupt glycaemic control and interact with diabetes medications, so preparation without added sugar glazes and with reduced soy sauce is recommended.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium (701mg/100g) elevates blood pressure risk; sesame oil provides beneficial MUFA/PUFA but cannot offset sodium burden
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should exercise caution with sesame oil chicken due to its high sodium content of 701mg/100g, which contributes to hypertension and adverse cardiovascular outcomes; the sesame oil component provides heart-beneficial monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fatty acids, but this benefit is outweighed by the sodium burden unless the dish is prepared with significantly reduced soy sauce.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sodium 701mg/100g exceeds the 400mg RA threshold, worsening joint oedema; alcohol is pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid standard sesame oil chicken due to sodium at 701mg/100g exceeding the 400mg RA threshold, which worsens joint oedema; ginger and sesame oil provide anti-inflammatory gingerols and MUFA/PUFA respectively, but these benefits cannot offset the sodium burden, and rice wine alcohol is pro-inflammatory and interacts with RA medications including methotrexate and biologics.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Sesame Oil Chicken (麻油鸡, Ma You Ji) is a classic Singaporean Chinese braised chicken dish prepared by simmering bone-in or boneless chicken pieces in fragrant sesame oil, Shaoxing rice wine, fresh ginger slices, and soy sauce. The dish is characterised by its deeply aromatic, nutty sesame fragrance, tender braised chicken texture, and a savoury-sweet braising liquor with warming ginger notes. It is a staple comfort dish in Singaporean Chinese households and is commonly served during confinement periods for postpartum recovery, as sesame oil and ginger are traditionally believed to promote warmth and circulation. Nutritionally, per 100g it provides 163 kcal, 16.87g protein, 8.22g fat, and 5.41g carbohydrate, with a notably high sodium content of 701mg driven by soy sauce. The high protein content supports satiety and muscle maintenance. The sesame oil contributes monounsaturated and polyunsaturated fatty acids with anti-inflammatory properties. Clinically, the high sodium content is the primary concern for individuals with hypertension, cardiovascular disease, and rheumatoid arthritis. For Type 2 Diabetes management, the low glycaemic index and modest carbohydrate load are favourable, though sodium and alcohol content require monitoring. The dish is halal-certified in most Singapore hawker and restaurant preparations when rice wine is omitted or substituted.
common_user_queries: How much sodium is in sesame oil chicken?
Can diabetics eat sesame oil chicken?
Is Ma You Ji suitable for hypertension?
What is sesame oil chicken made of?
Is sesame oil chicken high in protein?
Can I eat sesame oil chicken if I have high blood pressure?
What is the GI of sesame oil chicken?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database canonical entry for Sesame Oil Chicken (F11020047) with confidence 0.95. This is the primary approved source for Singaporean dishes. All core macros and secondary nutrients are exact matches from this source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient, and moisture data for this specific sesame oil chicken preparation are not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry F11020047 as of 2026. saturated_fat_g estimated at 1.8g/100g based on typical cooked chicken thigh fat composition from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 05165) as a supplementary reference, as HPB does not report saturated fat separately for this entry. GI value of 35 is an analogous estimate based on braised chicken dishes with soy-based seasonings from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for sesame oil chicken exists in the approved GI database. Halal status applies when rice wine is omitted or substituted with halal-certified cooking wine. The input record listed gi_value as 0 with low confidence (0.583) from an embedded GI database — this has been replaced with a defensible analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database.
